compiled_state.title: Conflict detection script should not flag .test-index as a concurrent modification conflict
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Problem
The file overlap / conflict detection script used by sprint orchestration (agent-batch-lifecycle.sh file-overlap or similar) flags .test-index as a conflict when multiple concurrent sub-agents modify it. .test-index is an auto-maintained mapping file (source → test associations) that the test gate self-heals via pruning. Multiple agents adding entries to it should not be treated as a blocking conflict — it is append-friendly and merge-trivial.

## Expected Behavior
.test-index should be excluded from file overlap conflict detection, similar to how .tickets/.index.json has a custom merge driver. Concurrent modifications to .test-index are harmless and should not prevent tasks from running in parallel.

## Suggested Fix
Add .test-index to an exclusion list in the conflict detection script so it is not counted as a file overlap.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 1 (BASIC). sprint-next-batch.sh file-overlap check has no exclusion list for shared-by-design files like .test-index. Fix: add OVERLAP_SAFE_FILES set and filter them out before the conflict check.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
